theme_and_vibe.description: Minimal/Dark theme with Vibrant/Colorful accents. Modern/Professional blended into an 'Elite Training Facility' / 'Cyber-academic' archetype. Prioritizes focus, data density, and high-tech security (hackerproof).

font_families.headings: Cabinet Grotesk or Clash Display (Professional, modern, slight tech edge)
font_families.body: IBM Plex Sans or JetBrains Mono (Tech-forward, tabular numbers for grades/stats)

rules: Headings MUST use tracking-tight or tracking-tighter.
Variable fonts preferred. Emphasize weight contrast.
Never center align default text, left align for readability.
Use dark gray/navy-tinted black for background, avoid pure #000000.
High contrast required: ensure APCA standards met.
Gradient accents allowed ONLY for primary interactive elements or data highlights.
Danger alert MUST be vibrant and instantly recognizable for the Anti-Cheat UI.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: Grid Borders (The Technical Look). Minimal box shadows; rely on 1px borders and slight inner glow or luminous active borders.
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Used only for floating sticky headers or modals. Backdrop blur heavy (blur-2xl) with 1px border-white/10.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise overlay on the background to prevent digital flatness.

layouts_and_grids.strategy: MODE B: High Density Grid for Dashboards.
rules: Base grid for dashboard: grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Tight gap (gap-4 or gap-6).
Always pad containers with borders to prevent collision (p-6 or p-8).
Maintain crisp edges, no excessive rounded corners (max radius md, 6px-8px).

components.general: Prefer Shadcn components heavily customized to match the dark neon tech theme. Override the Vercel look.
components.cards: Flat solid background, 1px solid border. Hover state: slight lift and border color brightens. DON'T overuse card component; go cardless or rectangular dense styles.
components.buttons: Sharp or slightly rounded corners, vibrant background on primary buttons, interactive hover states.
components.forms: Inputs should have flat backgrounds with visible borders. Focus state must glow with primary accent.
components.tables: Use for Assignments, Grades, and Admin Submissions. High contrast headers. Tabular font for numbers.
components.alerts: For Anti-cheat violations, use heavy visual warnings with danger color.

rules: Micro-animations on all interactive elements (hover, active).
Smooth layout transitions between dashboard tabs.
DO NOT apply `transition: all` globally. Apply transitions to specific properties.

image_urls.description: Abstract neon lights and geometric shapes
Person using track pad in dark room

rules: All interactive elements MUST include `data-testid` attribute (e.g., `data-testid="login-submit-button"`).
Support keyboard navigation and focus rings on all inputs/buttons.
Ensure color contrast meets WCAG AA/APCA standards.

GENERAL_UI_UX_DESIGN_GUIDELINES: CRITICAL: DO NOT use complete `transparent` background in header, overlays, any surface that has content under it. Example: Dropdown with transparent background.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
Use optical alignment over geometrical alignment.
Focus on negative space and contrast.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Avoid the 'AI slop' aesthetic by ensuring distinct hover states, sharp typography, and dense, functional layouts.
Do not center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; } is FORBIDDEN).
NEVER use emojis for icons. Use lucide-react.
Create a hackerproof environment: enforce route protection, prevent context menu / inspect element for non-admins.
Implement Supabase Auth properly with strict role-based access (student vs admin).
instructions_to_main_agent: Code Implementation Note: You are working with .js / .jsx files. Do not use TypeScript (.tsx).
Implement a unified login page with Supabase auth (Email/Password), routing to Student or Admin dashboard based on role.
Student Dashboard: Sections for Tests (MCQ), Assignments, Grades, Announcements.
Tests Section: When a test is opened, initiate the Anti-Cheat Tracker. Listen for `visibilitychange` or `blur` events. Increment a counter every time the tab loses focus.
Anti-Cheat Logic: If tab switch counter > 5, flag the test submission and record it in the database for the Admin.
Security: Prevent right-click (`contextmenu`) and common inspect shortcuts (F12, Ctrl+Shift+I) on the student portal using global event listeners.
Admin Dashboard: Create/manage tests, assignments. View submissions and a dedicated 'Violations' view displaying the tab switch counts per student per test.
Style the portal with a minimal dark theme, utilizing vibrant neon accents (cyan/purple) for primary buttons and severe red alerts for cheating warnings.
Use the Grid Borders pattern for data presentation in the Admin view to make it look like an elite analytical terminal.
